text:
COMPREHENSIVE SOLID TUMOR NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Patient Name: Margaret Yuen
Date of Birth: [REDACTED - 62 years of age at time of specimen collection]
MRN: [REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: [Attending Thoracic Oncologist, REDACTED]
Diagnosis (submitted): Non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, left upper lobe
Specimen ID: NGS-LUAD-[REDACTED]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue вҖ” CT-guided core needle biopsy, left upper lobe mass
Tumor Content (pathologist-estimated): 45% (adequate for sequencing)
Date Specimen Accessioned: [REDACTED]
Date Report Issued: [REDACTED]
Testing Laboratory: [REDACTED Molecular Diagnostics Laboratory, CLIA-certified, CAP-accredited]

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
METHODOLOGY
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Platform: Illumina NovaSeq 6000 (paired-end sequencing)
Assay: Comprehensive Solid Tumor Panel вҖ” 523-gene hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing assay
Genomic Targets: Single nucleotide variants (SNVs), small insertions/deletions (indels), copy number alterations (CNAs), gene fusions/rearrangements (RNA-based where applicable), and select splice variants
Coverage: Median sequencing depth вүҘ500x across targeted regions; hotspot regions вүҘ1000x
Limit of Detection: SNVs/indels вүҘ5% VAF at вүҘ500x depth; CNAs assessed by comparative genomic hybridization-like algorithms; fusions assessed by anchored multiplex PCR on extracted RNA
Germline Comparison: Matched normal (peripheral blood) sequencing performed in parallel to enable somatic/germline distinction
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): Calculated from synonymous and non-synonymous mutations across a 1.2 Mb evaluable genomic space
Microsatellite Instability (MSI): Evaluated at 100+ microsatellite loci by NGS
Quality Metrics: Specimen passed all internal QC metrics. Average coverage 584x. Tumor fraction confirmed above threshold.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
GENOMIC FINDINGS SUMMARY
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

SOURCE OF TRUTH: Tissue biopsy (FFPE), left upper lobe, core needle

                                                        (CNR 4.1)                    sensitizing mutation; FDA-approved targeted
                                                                                     therapy available (osimertinib, erlotinib,
                                                                                     gefitinib, afatinib, dacomitinib)

MET        вҖ”                            вҖ”       вҖ”       Amplified   Likely           Low-level amplification (CNR 2.4); MET
                                                        (CNR 2.4)   Pathogenic       amplification is associated with primary
                                                                                     resistance to EGFR TKI therapy and may
                                                                                     represent a co-existing bypass signaling
                                                                                     pathway; clinical significance in the context
                                                                                     of co-occurring EGFR L858R to be interpreted
                                                                                     with caution; low-level amplification warrants
                                                                                     monitoring at disease progression

STK11      p.Q37* (c.109C>T)            1       22%     вҖ”           Pathogenic       Nonsense (truncating) mutation; STK11/LKB1
                                                                                     loss-of-function; associated with immune-cold
                                                                                     tumor microenvironment, resistance to PD-1/
                                                                                     PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitor therapy, and
                                                                                     poorer prognosis in NSCLC; no approved
                                                                                     targeted therapy; investigational strategies
                                                                                     under evaluation

KEAP1      p.G480V (c.1439G>T)          вҖ”       19%     вҖ”           Likely           Missense mutation affecting Kelch domain;
                                                                     Pathogenic       KEAP1 loss-of-function leads to NRF2
                                                                                     pathway activation and oxidative stress
                                                                                     response dysregulation; co-occurring with
                                                                                     STK11 mutation, a combination associated
                                                                                     with particularly poor immunotherapy response
                                                                                     and aggressive disease biology in LUAD

                                                                                     dominant-negative TP53 alteration; frequent
                                                                                     co-mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC; associated
                                                                                     with genomic instability; no directly
                                                                                     actionable targeted therapy

                                                                                     suppressors; associated with CDK4/6
                                                                                     pathway dysregulation and cell cycle
                                                                                     checkpoint loss; CDK4/6 inhibitor
                                                                                     investigation ongoing in NSCLC; not
                                                                                     standard of care in this histology

NEGATIVE / NOT DETECTED FINDINGS
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў KRAS: No mutations detected (codons 12, 13, 61, 117, 146)
вҖў EGFR exon 19 deletion: Not detected
вҖў EGFR exon 20 insertion: Not detected
вҖў EGFR T790M (exon 20): Not detected (baseline вҖ” absence at diagnosis as expected)
вҖў EGFR C797S: Not detected
вҖў BRAF V600E and non-V600 mutations: Not detected
вҖў ALK gene rearrangement/fusion: Not detected (confirmed by both DNA-level break-apart analysis and RNA fusion panel)
вҖў ROS1 gene rearrangement/fusion: Not detected
вҖў RET gene rearrangement/fusion: Not detected
вҖў MET exon 14 skipping mutation: Not detected
вҖў ERBB2 (HER2) amplification or activating mutation: Not detected
вҖў NTRK1/2/3 gene fusions: Not detected
вҖў PIK3CA: No mutations detected
вҖў PTEN: No mutations detected
вҖў SMAD4: No mutations detected
вҖў NF1: No mutations detected
вҖў MYC amplification: Not detected
вҖў FGFR1/2/3 amplification or fusion: Not detected

BIOMARKER SUMMARY

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
INTERPRETATION AND CLINICAL COMMENTARY
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
This 62-year-old female with Stage IIIB lung adenocarcinoma harbors an EGFR exon 21 L858R activating point mutation (VAF 38%) as the dominant oncogenic driver. This is the most common EGFR sensitizing mutation (comprising approximately 40-45% of all EGFR-mutant NSCLCs) and is associated with robust clinical responses to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors. Notably, EGFR amplification is also present (CNR 4.1), which may serve to further amplify oncogene signaling.

The mutational co-landscape is notable and clinically important:

1. STK11 p.Q37* (VAF 22%): This early truncating nonsense mutation results in complete loss of LKB1 kinase function. STK11/LKB1 is a master regulator of cellular metabolism and is a well-validated tumor suppressor in NSCLC. STK11 mutations co-occur in approximately 15-25% of KRAS-mutant and a smaller fraction of EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinomas. Loss of LKB1 reprograms the tumor microenvironment toward an immunosuppressed, neutrophil-rich state, which has been consistently associated with primary resistance to PD-1 and PD-L1 checkpoint blockade. While the patient's PD-L1 TPS is 15% (low positive), the STK11 mutation substantially diminishes the expected benefit from immunotherapy.

2. KEAP1 p.G480V (VAF 19%): KEAP1 encodes a negative regulator of NRF2, the master transcription factor for antioxidant and cytoprotective gene expression. Loss-of-function KEAP1 mutations lead to constitutive NRF2 activation, conferring resistance to oxidative stress (including that induced by chemotherapy and radiation), and have been independently associated with poor outcomes in NSCLC. The concurrent presence of both STK11 and KEAP1 mutations in a single tumor вҖ” as seen here вҖ” represents a particularly unfavorable co-mutation signature in lung adenocarcinoma, strongly predicting resistance to immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy and associated with a more aggressive clinical course.

3. TP53 p.R248W (VAF 31%): This is one of the most common gain-of-function TP53 hotspot mutations (R248W and R248Q are the most frequently mutated TP53 codons in human cancer). Dominant-negative TP53 mutations disrupt wild-type p53 tetramer function and may contribute to genomic instability. Co-mutation of TP53 with EGFR in lung adenocarcinoma is common (~30-40% of EGFR-mutant cases) and does not substantially alter EGFR TKI sensitivity in most studies.

4. CDKN2A homozygous deletion: Loss of CDKN2A abrogates both p16/INK4A (CDK4/6 inhibitory) and p14/ARF (MDM2-p53 regulatory) functions. In the context of the co-occurring TP53 mutation, complete loss of this checkpoint axis may contribute to accelerated cell cycle progression.

5. MET low-level amplification (CNR 2.4): This finding is at the borderline of interpretive significance. True high-level MET amplification (CNR вүҘ5) is a known primary resistance mechanism to EGFR TKI therapy. At this level, the clinical significance is less certain вҖ” polysomy of chromosome 7 (which harbors both EGFR at 7p11.2 and MET at 7q31.2) could account for a co-amplification pattern. This finding should be reassessed at the time of any disease progression on EGFR TKI therapy, as clonal selection pressure may drive emergence of high-level MET amplification as an acquired resistance mechanism.

IMPORTANT NEGATIVE FINDINGS: The absence of KRAS, BRAF, ALK, ROS1, RET, MET exon 14 skipping, ERBB2, and NTRK alterations is consistent with classical EGFR-driven adenocarcinoma biology. The absence of T790M at baseline is expected. TMB is low (6 mut/Mb), consistent with the relatively low mutational burden typical of EGFR-mutant NSCLC in former light smokers. No germline pathogenic variants were identified.

CLINICAL TRIAL CONSIDERATIONS
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
Based on the genomic profile identified in this specimen, the following categories of clinical trial investigation may be relevant:

вҖў EGFR-mutant NSCLC, 3rd-generation TKI combinations (e.g., osimertinib plus anti-VEGF, anti-MET, or CDK4/6 inhibitor strategies)
вҖў STK11/KEAP1 co-mutated NSCLC вҖ” NRF2 pathway inhibition, metabolic reprogramming strategies, novel immune sensitization approaches
вҖў MET co-amplified EGFR-mutant NSCLC вҖ” combination EGFR TKI + MET inhibitor strategies (investigational in low-level amplification)
вҖў CDKN2A-deleted NSCLC вҖ” CDK4/6 inhibitor combinations

Providers are encouraged to consult ClinicalTrials.gov or contact the institution's molecular tumor board for current trial matching.

вҖў This assay detects somatic alterations above established limits of detection. Variants below the sensitivity threshold may not be detected.
вҖў Tumor heterogeneity may result in underrepresentation of subclonal alterations in the biopsy specimen analyzed.
вҖў Copy number alterations in FFPE specimens are subject to methodological limitations; low-level amplifications (CNR 2вҖ“3) should be interpreted with clinical correlation and, where available, orthogonal confirmation (e.g., FISH or repeat biopsy at progression).
вҖў Germline findings are reported only for variants meeting pathogenicity criteria in the matched blood specimen; variants of uncertain significance in germline are not reported.
вҖў This report should be interpreted in the context of all available clinical, pathological, and radiographic information by a qualified medical professional.
вҖў Results from this assay are intended to complement, not replace, established clinical diagnostic and therapeutic algorithms.
